must: Read the full target content before making any decision
Read every file listed in 'Context to read' before touching anything in this stage
Read PLAN.md as the single source of truth — do NOT read PREPLAN.md
must_not: Assume missing details without checking source files
Skip reading dependency files listed in 'Context to read'

must: Create only files explicitly listed in files_in_scope
For PACKAGE_1 only: execute 'git add . && git commit -m "chore: save state before Plan execution"' as step 0 before any file changes
must_not: Create any file not listed in '## Files to modify' of the active package
Touch, edit, or refactor any file not listed in the active package scope

must: Preserve existing contracts unless the stage explicitly changes them
Read the file before editing it
Touch ONLY files listed in files_in_scope
must_not: Touch, edit, or refactor any file not listed in '## Files to modify' of the active package

must: Verify no other module imports the file before deleting
Run the full test suite from all prior packages that depended on this file after deletion
must_not: Delete a file that is still imported elsewhere without updating all call-sites first

must: Run the specified test command and report exact output
Report the result immediately via test.report — do not delay
For small issues (typos, missing imports, syntax): attempt exactly ONE fix, then if still failing — generate DEBUG HANDOFF and stop
For complex logic or structural issues: do NOT attempt any fix — generate DEBUG HANDOFF immediately and stop
must_not: Advance stage when test result is FAIL
Attempt more than ONE fix for any failing test
Run the final sequence if tests are failing
Skip running the test command at the end of every stage

must: Execute git commands exactly as provided in git_instruction from the MCP response
Execute git commands in the exact order specified
must_not: Modify the git command provided by MCP
Skip the git commit after a PASS test report

must: Follow package constraints and stop conditions verbatim
Touch only files listed for the active package
Call guard.report before test.report — every stage, no exceptions
After completing a package: update EXECUTION_LOG.md first, then git commit, then open next package file in VS Code as the absolute last action
When generating DEBUG HANDOFF: include plan name, package file path, resume point, and automated_run flag
must_not: Skip required sequence: guard.report → test.report → stage.advance
Read, write, or interact with any file after executing the 'code' command to open the next package
Chain multiple fixes — one change, then tests, then report
Proceed to the next stage if the current stage test is failing

templates.EDIT+TEST:
1. READ all files_in_scope
2. EDIT as specified
3. TEST: run test command
4. If FAIL (small issue): one fix attempt → re-run → if still FAIL → DEBUG HANDOFF → STOP
5. If FAIL (complex): DEBUG HANDOFF immediately → STOP
6. If PASS: call guard.report → test.report → await git_instruction
templates.CREATE+TEST:
1. READ all context_to_read files
2. CREATE files as specified
3. TEST: run test command
4. If FAIL (small issue): one fix attempt → re-run → if still FAIL → DEBUG HANDOFF → STOP
5. If FAIL (complex): DEBUG HANDOFF immediately → STOP
6. If PASS: call guard.report → test.report → await git_instruction
templates.READ+EDIT+TEST:
1. READ all files_in_scope and context_to_read
2. EDIT as specified
3. TEST: run test command
4. If FAIL (small issue): one fix attempt → re-run → if still FAIL → DEBUG HANDOFF → STOP
5. If FAIL (complex): DEBUG HANDOFF immediately → STOP
6. If PASS: call guard.report → test.report → await git_instruction
templates.CREATE+EDIT+TEST:
1. READ all context_to_read files
2. CREATE and EDIT as specified
3. TEST: run test command
4. If FAIL (small issue): one fix attempt → re-run → if still FAIL → DEBUG HANDOFF → STOP
5. If FAIL (complex): DEBUG HANDOFF immediately → STOP
6. If PASS: call guard.report → test.report → await git_instruction
